content.cn_text: 海胆遍布海底，分布范围广泛，但很少出现在

content.en_text: water, and sea urchins are also commonly found living coral reefs, and sea urchin mainly

content.cn_text: 以珊瑚和岩石上的植物为食。
content.en_text: feeds on plants on coral and rocks.

content.cn_text: 它们被许多生活在海洋环境中的捕食者捕食。
content.en_text: They are preyed by many predators that live their marine environment.

content.cn_text: 海胆身体呈圆形，长有从身上伸出的棘刺，这些棘刺
content.en_text: Sea urchins have around shaped the body, with the lost spines, that come off it, which

content.cn_text: 用于保护、移动以及捕捉水中漂浮的食物颗粒。
content.en_text: are used for protection, to move around, and to jump food particles floating around in

content.cn_text: 水中。
content.en_text: the water.

content.cn_text: 海胆有五排细小的管足。
content.en_text: Sea urchins have five bare-to-roes of tiny tube of feet.

content.cn_text: 海胆的管足有吸盘，帮助海胆移动、捕食和
content.en_text: The feet of sea urchin have suckers which help sea urchin to move, capture food, and

content.cn_text: 附着在海底。
content.en_text: to hold onto the ocean floor.

content.cn_text: 海胆曾一度被认为是害虫，因为它们大量捕食海带，严重影响了
content.en_text: Urchins will once consider a pest, because their large habitat for kelp severely affected

content.cn_text: 海带的收成，但在20世纪70年代，当人们发现它有另一种用途，即作为
content.en_text: the kelp harvest, but this changed in the 1970s when another use was discovered, as

content.cn_text: 美味的食物时，这种情况发生了改变。
content.en_text: a delicious food.

content.cn_text: 通过适当的加工和连夜运送到市场，海胆再次
content.en_text: With access to proper processing and overnight shipping to markets, the sea urchin got

content.cn_text: 迅速走红。
content.en_text: outrushed again.

content.cn_text: 海胆现在是加利福尼亚州最大的海洋出口产品，这得益于
content.en_text: Urchins are now California's largest export item from the ocean, thanks to the steady market

content.cn_text: 亚洲的稳定市场。
content.en_text: from Asia.

content.cn_text: 估计有10,000或更多的人直接或间接地从加利福尼亚州的海胆产业中
content.en_text: They are estimated 10,000 or more people who provide income for their families, either

content.cn_text: 为他们的家庭提供收入。
content.en_text: directly or indirectly from the sea urchin industry in the state of California.

content.cn_text: 由于日本对美国海胆的强烈需求，今天这种生物被如此猛烈地捕捞，以至于
content.en_text: Due to intense demand for US sea urchin in Japan, today the creature is so aggressively

content.cn_text: 以至于科学家们开始担心其种群数量实际上已经下降，
content.en_text: haunted that scientists have began to worry about the number of population actually declined,

content.cn_text: 这使得海胆供应面临危险。
content.en_text: which is in danger in the sea urchin supply.

content.cn_text: 在加利福尼亚州，它现在是最大的海洋出口产品，每年价值超过7500万美元，
content.en_text: In California, it is now the largest export item from the ocean, valued at over 75 million

content.cn_text: 其中大部分来自日本。
content.en_text: dollars per year, most of which comes from Japan.

content.cn_text: 海胆是一种非凡的生物，它们可以迅速修复棘刺和
content.en_text: Sea urchins are remarkable organisms that can quickly record damage to the spines and

content.cn_text: 管足的损伤。
content.en_text: feet.

content.cn_text: 有些物种也活到非凡的高龄，更值得注意的是，它们没有衰老的迹象，
content.en_text: Some species also live extraordinary old age, and even more remarkably no sign of aging,

content.cn_text: 例如再生能力下降或与亚洲相关的不朽性增加（此句转录有误，原意应为“age-related mortality”，即年龄相关的死亡率）。
content.en_text: such as decline in regenerative capacity or increase in Asia-related immortality.

content.cn_text: 在最近发表于《衰老细胞》杂志上的一篇论文中，科学家们揭示了海胆的
content.en_text: In a paper recently published in the Aging Cell, the scientist has shown new light on

content.cn_text: 衰老过程，这表明通常伴随衰老而来的身体机能下降
content.en_text: the process in sea urchins, bracing the prospects of the physical decline that typically

content.cn_text: 并非不可避免。
content.en_text: accompanied aging is not inevitable.

content.cn_text: 这项新研究得出结论，海胆这种生活在浅水区的小型多刺生物
content.en_text: The new study has concluded that sea urchin, a small spiny creature that lives in the shallow

content.cn_text: 是地球上寿命最长的动物之一。
content.en_text: coastal waters, is among the longest living animals on Earth.

content.cn_text: 它们可以活到100岁，有些甚至可以健康地活到200年。
content.en_text: They can live to be 100 years old and some may reach 200 years in good health.

content.cn_text: 事实上，幼年时的红海胆生长得相当快。
content.en_text: The red sea urchin, in fact, does grow fairly quickly when it's young.

content.cn_text: 两岁时，它一年内可以从2厘米长到4厘米。
content.en_text: At the age of two years, it can grow from 2 centimeters to 4 centimeters in one year.

content.cn_text: 当海胆进入青少年时期，它的生长速度显著减慢。
content.en_text: By the time the sea urchin is a teenager, it grows slowly, dramatically.

content.cn_text: 研究人员发现，22岁时，它每年大约从12厘米
content.en_text: At the age of 22, researchers have found it to grow each year from about 12 centimeters

content.cn_text: 只长到12.1厘米，这意味着比前一年只大了0.1厘米。
content.en_text: to only 12.1 centimeters, which means only 0.1 centimeters bigger than a year before.

content.cn_text: 但令人惊讶的是，它似乎从未真正停止生长，只是非常非常缓慢。
content.en_text: But somewhat remarkably, it appears never really stop growing, it's just very, very slow.

content.cn_text: 有证据表明，100岁的红海胆和10岁的海胆一样，有能力再活
content.en_text: The evidence suggests that 100-year-old red sea urchin is just as apt to live another

content.cn_text: 一年或进行繁殖。
content.en_text: year or produce as a 10-year-old sea urchin.

content.cn_text: 事实上，更成熟的红海胆似乎是精子和卵子的生产者，并且
content.en_text: The more mature red sea urchins, in fact, appear to be the producers of sperm and eggs and

content.cn_text: 即使非常年迈也完全能够繁殖。
content.en_text: are perfectly able to breed even when incredibly old.

content.cn_text: 碳-14测年法在特定情况下提供了非常有力且独立的生长速率和年龄测试。
content.en_text: Radio-carbon testing in its time of a situation provided a very strong and independent test of

content.cn_text: 生长速率和年龄。
content.en_text: growth rates and age.

content.cn_text: 此外，它证实了老年海胆的生长非常稳定、非常
content.en_text: Among other things, it confirms that in older sea urchins, there's a very steady, very

content.cn_text: 持续，并且与海洋条件或其他变量完全无关。
content.en_text: consistent growth as quite independent of ocean conditions or other variables.

content.cn_text: 一旦它们接近成年大小，我们的研究表明它们就没有生长。
content.en_text: And once they're near the adult size, our research indicates that they do not have growth.

content.cn_text: 对于这些物种来说，这很简单。
content.en_text: With the speeches, it's pretty simple.

content.cn_text: 它们越大，就越老。
content.en_text: The bigger they are, the older they are.

content.cn_text: 它们越大，就越成熟，红海胆越成熟。
content.en_text: The bigger they are, the more mature red sea urchins, the more mature red sea urchins are.
